Code fragment: public final TableField<BenutzerScoreBoardRecord, String> BENUTZERID = createField(DSL.name("benutzerId"), SQLDataType.VARCHAR.nullable(false), this, "");
Explanation: The 'benutzerId' column appears to reference the 'Benutzer' table but lacks a foreign key constraint, which could lead to orphaned records or referential integrity issues. Consider adding a foreign key constraint referencing the composite primary key (TENANT, ID) of the Benutzer table, similar to how BENUTZER_AUSZEICHNUNGEN__BENUTZER_AUSZEICHNUNGEN_FK is defined.

Code fragment: public final TableField<LiedRecord, String> BESITZER_ID = createField(DSL.name("besitzer_id"), SQLDataType.VARCHAR, this, "");
Explanation: The column 'besitzer_id' appears to reference a 'Benutzer' (user/owner) but lacks a foreign key constraint to enforce referential integrity. Consider adding a composite foreign key on (TENANT, BESITZER_ID) referencing PK_TENANT_BENUTZER to ensure data consistency.

Using `selectFrom(LIED)` fetches all columns from the LIED table, including the potentially large BYTES column, when only ID, TITEL, BESITZER_ID, and TENANT are needed. Replace with an explicit `select(LIED.ID, LIED.TITEL, LIED.BESITZER_ID, LIED.TENANT).from(LIED)` to avoid fetching unnecessary data.

Using selectFrom(BENUTZER) fetches all columns from the BENUTZER table into a record, even though only specific fields (ID, NAME, PASSWORT, EMAIL) are used. Consider using select() with only the required columns instead.

Using selectFrom(BENUTZER_AUSZEICHNUNGEN) fetches all columns from the table, but only the AUSZEICHNUNG column is used. Consider using select(BENUTZER_AUSZEICHNUNGEN.AUSZEICHNUNG) to fetch only the needed column.
